title: Turkish-Inspired Chicken Pilaf
description: A flavorful Indian-inspired dish that combines the richness of Turkish spices with the comfort of a pilaf.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, and salt, and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the pilaf rice and cook for 2-3 minutes, until lightly toasted.
Add the flavorful spice blend and turkish paprika, and stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
Serve with additional turkish paprika on top, if desired.
